analysis.priorityItem: Build cross-adapter worker lifecycle integration scenarios
analysis.description: Implement an integration test suite that runs the full cloud worker lifecycle for run/auto/review workflows against both Postgres and Turso backends. Cover successful execution plus cancel, lease_lost, and profile_revoked paths, and assert deterministic run/attempt transitions, cleanup behavior, and artifact restoration.
analysis.rationale: The worker orchestration pipeline is newly shipped and has the highest regression risk because it combines complex state transitions with adapter-specific claim semantics. End-to-end cross-adapter lifecycle coverage is high impact, directly recommended in the report, and well-defined enough to implement immediately using existing integration harness patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: A new integration-tagged worker lifecycle suite exists and runs against both Postgres and Turso store configurations.
The suite executes shared lifecycle scenarios for workflow kinds run, auto, and review using one common command/runner flow definition.
Successful scenarios verify terminal success state, persisted snapshot references, and that pull restores expected artifacts.
Cancel scenarios verify cancel intent transitions runs to canceled deterministically and attempt terminalization occurs exactly once.
Lease-loss scenarios verify expected terminal status/reason and no duplicate attempt terminalization is recorded.
Profile-revoked scenarios verify expected terminal status/reason and cleanup tolerates already-released auth locks (ErrNotFound) without failing terminalization.
Status/logs/pull JSON outputs in these scenarios are asserted for required camelCase contract keys.
A dedicated make target executes this integration suite and is used by CI or documented as the canonical invocation.
